channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Republicans Again Block War Powers Measure in the Senate
item.description: Four G.O.P. senators crossed party lines to back the resolution to direct President Trump to halt the war in Iran and seek authorization from Congress, but their support was not enough.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: The vote on Tuesday reflected a determination among Democrats to continue aggressively pushing back on President Trump’s handling of the war with Iran.

item.title: After U.S. Strike on Iranian School, Months Pass Without Answers
item.description: U.S. officials have not publicly acknowledged responsibility for the deaths or released a report on their findings from an investigation into the Feb. 28 strike.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Health care workers holding photos of children killed by airstrikes on the Shajarah Tayyebeh elementary school in Minab, Iran. At least 175 people were killed, according to Iranian officials.

item.title: Shipping companies remain cautious about the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural in Tehran in April depicting the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: War Loomed Over Past World Cups, Too
item.description: Here’s a look back at times when international conflicts seeped into soccer’s biggest tournament.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iranian fans during the World Cup match between Iran and New Zealand at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on Monday.

item.title: Iran Will Enter Nuclear Talks With the Trump Administration Feeling Emboldened
item.description: Despite military setbacks during the war, Tehran is presenting a narrative of victory before negotiations with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural showing Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, left, and Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, former supreme leaders of Iran, in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Oil Prices Continue to Fall as Traders Assess U.S.-Iran Deal
item.description: Stocks were mixed after strong rallies the day before.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times

item.title: France, Britain and Other Countries Say They’ll Send Ships to the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: For months, a coalition led by Britain and France has been preparing to send minesweepers and other ships to secure the strait once the fighting ends. That moment may finally be here.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The German minesweeper Fulda may be deployed to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Gas Prices May Remain High Despite US-Iran Deal
item.description: The preliminary agreement may not have an immediate effect on prices at the pump. Damaged infrastructure and risky transport could keep costs up.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have soared since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak

item.title: Why Team Iran Is in a Tough Spot at the World Cup
item.description: At its first game in Los Angeles, the men’s national team drew spectators who weren’t coming for the soccer, but rather to protest the regime in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Fans holding Iran’s pre-revolutionary flag at SoFi Stadium near Los Angeles on Monday.

item.title: Naval Mines Could Still Stymie Gulf Shipping After War
item.description: The U.S. Navy will be deploying a new generation of drone-based countermeasures to search the sea floor and surface for any mines Iran laid.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels trapped in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman.

item.title: In the Dark on U.S.-Iran Deal, Senators Refrain From Praising It
item.description: Democrats demanded an immediate briefing and even Republicans conceded they had no information on an agreement the administration has declined to release.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Michael Gold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Thune of South Dakota, the majority leader, said that he had not yet seen the deal but said he expected the administration to convene with lawmakers as the process continues.

item.title: Netanyahu Says Israel Will Keep Forces in Lebanon, Despite U.S.-Iran Deal
item.description: “The struggle has not ended,” a defiant Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told Israelis.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel speaking at a news conference in Jerusalem on Monday.

item.title: The (Mostly) Unanswered Questions at the Heart of a U.S.-Iran Accord
item.description: The short-term truce hasn’t been made public, and the long-term peace deal it is supposed to yield doesn’t exist yet, so there is much still unknown about any agreement between the adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Richard Pérez-Peña
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at Évian-les-Bains, France, for the Group of 7 summit, on Monday.

item.title: Will Commercial Ships Have to Pay to Pass Through the Strait of Hormuz? Maybe.
item.description: Charging a toll is illegal under international law, but some fees are allowed for services. It is not clear what services Iran would provide, but there were no fees charged before the war.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, seen from Oman on Wednesday.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Sign a Framework Deal, Leaving Major Issues for Future Talks
item.description: U.S. and Iranian officials said the deal included a 60-day cease-fire to pave the way for negotiations toward a final peace agreement and talks on Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Erica L. Green, Max Bearak and Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump with President Emmanuel Macron in Évian-les-Bains, France, on Monday for the Group of 7 summit. Mr. Macron called the agreement with Iran “a very important step toward peace.”

item.title: Jaded and Exhausted, Many Iranians Shrug at World Cup
item.description: The successes of Iran’s national soccer team used to be a source of celebration. Now, many Iranians can barely muster the energy to care.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi, Shirin Hakim and Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Members of Iran’s national soccer team training in Carson, Calif., on Sunday. Many Iranians say their problems are too big these days for them to care much about how the team does.

item.title: Gulf Countries Confront Questions About Relying on U.S. for Protection
item.description: Iran targeted Bahrain, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates and other American allies in the Middle East during the war, harming their economies and military sectors.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The sun setting in Kuwait last week. Iran has attacked the oil-rich country and other U.S. allies in the Middle East during the war.

item.title: Why the U.S.-Iran Deal Might Work, and Why It Might Not
item.description: For all of the cautious optimism surrounding the framework agreement, some of the toughest issues lie ahead, and the deal could still fall apart.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: The preliminary deal between Iran and the U.S. could still come apart.

item.title: Trump Winds Down the War He Started With Goals Unmet
item.description: While the president says the agreement with Iran would open the Strait of Hormuz and provide economic relief, the country’s nuclear program is still a subject for negotiation.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said on social media that the deal would “let the oil flow.”

item.title: Israel Counts the Ways That Netanyahu’s Iran Strategy Failed
item.description: The U.S.-Iran framework agreement appears to omit some of the most important provisions that Israel wanted.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel speaking in Jerusalem in April.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Agreement Leaves Lebanon’s Fate Murky
item.description: Iran claimed the deal would extend to fighting in Lebanon but no details of the agreement were made public and Israel said its forces would remain there.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Emergency response workers and security personnel at the site of an Israeli airstrike, in southern Beirut, Lebanon, on Sunday.

item.title: Deal to Reopen Hormuz Kicks Off Long Effort to Ease Energy Crisis
item.description: The pace of the recovery will depend on how confident companies are that the deal between the United States and Iran will hold and be extended.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz this month.

item.title: The U.S.-Iran Deal: What to Know
item.description: The full text of the deal that could pave the way to ending the war has not been published. Initial details suggest that it defers the most contentious issues.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Women in Tehran passing a mural showing Iranian missiles attacking an American ship.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Find Ways to Claim Victory on the Nuclear Issue
item.description: President Trump said Iran had promised to suspend enriching uranium, but it is unclear for how long.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Natanz nuclear complex in Iran, in March in an image provided by Vantor, a satellite imaging company.

item.title: Is This the End of Political Islam?
item.description: For decades, Islamic governance held allure in the Middle East. Now some scholars say the Islamist wave has passed.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: Middle East and North Africa Unrest (2010- )
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: A damaged photograph of Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini at the headquarters of Iran’s state TV broadcaster in Tehran after an Israeli strike last year.

item.title: Iranian Team Arrives in U.S. for World Cup After Months of Uncertainty
item.description: The war had stirred doubts about the team’s participation in the tournament. It is set to play New Zealand in a Los Angeles area stadium on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran’s players arriving at a stadium near Los Angeles on Sunday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Erica L. Green, Max Bearak, Johnatan Reiss and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: Many Iranians Express Relief Over Agreement to End the War
item.description: After enduring months of conflict, ordinary people in Iran were relieved to hear about the deal. Opposition groups were disappointed.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: World leaders welcome news that the Strait of Hormuz could soon reopen.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

item.title: U.S. and Iran Reach Agreement to Reopen Strait and Begin Nuclear Talks
item.description: The accord halts hostilities in the war that began in February but leaves unresolved the critical issue of Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gathering in Tehran earlier this month., Iran affirmed in the agreement that it would never seek to build or procure a nuclear weapon, but the regime has made that promise many times previously.

item.title: Trump Claims Strait Will Be ‘Permanently Toll-Free’ Under Agreement With Iran
item.description: In a call to The New York Times, President Trump praised Russia’s and China’s leaders and described Israel’s prime minister as “a very difficult guy.”
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump insisted on Sunday that if Iran failed to reach a final nuclear accord with the United States, he would restart military attacks on Tehran.

item.title: In Israel, Broad Discontent Even Before Deal’s Details Are Known
item.description: Israelis across the political spectrum have said the agreement appears to leave fundamental security threats posed by Iran unaddressed.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in March. At the start of the recent war, he had said that its objective was “to remove the existential threats” to Israel.

item.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Outskirts as Fighting With Hezbollah Escalates
item.description: The attacks complicated an already delicate moment as President Trump and Iran appeared to be edging toward finalizing a framework peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The site of an Israeli strike in Beirut’s southern outskirts on Sunday. Lebanese health authorities said three people were killed and 16 injured.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Adam Rasgon, Ronen Bergman and Jonathan Swan

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan and Abdi Latif Dahir

item.title: With a Deal Seemingly Close, the U.S. Faces an Iran More Willing to Withstand Pressure
item.description: Iran’s new, more militaristic leaders have already survived the worst that America and Israel can deliver, and seem readier to take risks.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps in April at a government-organized march in Tehran.

item.title: U.S. Says Iran Cease-Fire Deal ‘Very Close’
item.description: A senior administration official said the two sides were “not quite at the finish line yet.”
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: As Speculation Swirls About the Emerging Iran Deal, Here’s What We Know
item.description: A “memorandum of understanding” between the two sides is under discussion. Both sides are keen to frame it as a victory.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Israel Is Building Army Base in Jenin, Flouting 1990s Pact With Palestinians
item.description: Critics say the move would help the Israeli military protect increased settlement activity near Palestinian population centers in the West Bank.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Adam Rasgon
item.media:credit: Afif Amireh for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Israeli military has held part of Jenin, in the occupied West Bank, since January of last year.

item.title: Despite Talk of an Iran Peace Deal, Lebanon’s War Grinds On
item.description: Israeli strikes on Friday left Lebanon out of sync with a cautious optimism taking hold elsewhere in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Residents survey damage following an Israeli airstrike in the southern port city of Tyre on Friday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Farnaz Fassihi and Adam Rasgon

item.title: Risk Strait of Hormuz or Wait? Shipping Companies Face a Costly Dilemma.
item.description: With more than 500 ships still stranded in the Persian Gulf, pressure on the shipowners and sailors is growing by the day.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Supplies of fresh food and water have been running low for the roughly 11,000 sailors stuck on hundreds of ships anchored in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: A Dangerous Limbo Leaves Iran, and the World, Between Peace and War
item.description: Since announcing a nominal cease-fire two months ago, Iran, Israel and the U.S. have remained locked in low-intensity violence that has become a new normal.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler and Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: In Tehran on Monday, a billboard featuring the Iran theocracy’s first two supreme leaders loomed over passers-by.

item.title: The World Is Draining Oil Reserves, Raising Pressure for a Peace Deal
item.description: The amount of oil and fuel stored by businesses and governments has fallen sharply since the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Lazaro Gamio
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
